resource_limits.oom_counter_detail: Docker Desktop exposes cgroup event files but denies sandbox reads; NemoClaw status reports the optional metric as unavailable.

verified_cases: Host, Docker bridge, host DNS, and container DNS preflight
OpenShell gateway health
Managed inference route HTTP health
Deep Agents Code managed-launcher identity
Exact process and file-descriptor limits
Live model invocation returned OPEN_AGENT_MODEL_OK
Git-root project profile discovery loaded orchestrator, researcher, and agent-retrieval skill
Temporary HTTPS MCP registration and credential-resolution control returned HTTP 200/401
Tightened OpenShell MCP handshake and tool method policy loaded at version 43
OpenShell denied resources/list with HTTP 403 before server dispatch
Fixture rejected an unknown tool and malformed arguments without handler dispatch
Orchestrator delegated once; researcher searched and fetched five-layer-stack with exact provenance
Direct live injection evaluation preserved provenance, called no sentinel tool, and revealed no credential
Provider environment uses a synthetic gateway credential; MCP uses an OpenShell resolution placeholder; workspace provider-key scan is clean
Managed launcher rejected a headless shell allow-list override before agent startup
Interactive reject caused no execution; interactive approve executed the exact command once
The persisted Deep Agents thread resumed by identifier after the terminal exited
A one-second agent deadline failed cleanly with exit code 124
Temporary MCP adapter, credential provider, policy endpoint, uploaded workspace, fixture, tunnel, and bearer token were removed
A later registration-failure injection left no MCP bridge, uploaded workspace, fixture process, tunnel process, or credential
Stable Cloudflare Worker health returned HTTP 200 and unauthenticated MCP returned HTTP 401
Three consecutive credential probes and LangChain MCP sessions passed before inference
Persisted stable-endpoint trace proved one delegation, one managed search, one canonical fetch, exact provenance, and seven tool calls within budget
Permanent research bridge is attached and credential-ready; a direct LangChain session discovered exactly research.search and research.fetch
Effective permanent OpenShell policy version 47 permits only five MCP methods with a 16 KiB body cap
blockers: The sandbox image digest is not exposed by the current OpenShell status surfaces and remains unpinned.

The full Deep Agents injection path was interrupted by the NVIDIA worker request quota; a direct pinned model-plus-fixture containment check passed, but the end-to-end case must still be rerun before release.
The complete live budget and dependency-failure matrices remain; the agent deadline path passed.
A disposable sandbox rebuild/destroy lifecycle check remains; the configured sandbox was preserved.

NemoClaw records its broader generated MCP policy, so the reviewed tighter effective policy intentionally appears as drift and prevents the built-in post-tightening credential probe; the deployment separately verifies the actual LangChain session and effective policy.
